evals.prompt: Build a login page with two options: sign in with Google, or enter an email to receive a magic link. After the link is sent, show a confirmation message. If the user is already authenticated, redirect them to the dashboard.
evals.expected_output: A login page using useGoogleLogin() for OAuth and useSendLoginLink() for magic link, with redirect logic using useEffect + useNavigate.
expectations: Uses useGoogleLogin() for the Google sign-in URL
Uses useSendLoginLink() for magic link — not a custom fetch
Shows toast.success() or inline confirmation after link is sent
Redirects authenticated users with useNavigate + getPageUrl, not window.location
Does not invent a password field or custom auth implementation

evals.prompt: This app has two roles: Admin and Employee. Build the layout so Admins see a navigation with Dashboard, Users, Reports, and Settings, while Employees only see Dashboard and their own Profile. Both see a user menu with logout.
evals.expected_output: Role-based layout branching on user.role, each role getting the correct nav items. User menu present on both. useUser() used correctly.
expectations: Uses useUser() to get the current user and their role
Admin nav includes Dashboard, Users, Reports, Settings
Employee nav includes only Dashboard and Profile
Both layouts include a user menu with logout via logOut() from @/lib/utils
Does not create a role-switcher UI or store role in state

evals.prompt: Show the current user's full name, email, role, and a profile picture (falling back to their initials if no picture). Add a logout button. This is a standalone profile widget, not a full page.
evals.expected_output: A component using useUser(), Avatar with AvatarImage + AvatarFallback, and logOut(). Shows all requested user fields.
expectations: Uses AvatarImage with profileImageUrl when available, AvatarFallback with initials otherwise
